content: Comparative studies of intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM) and random network distillation (RND) for curiosity-driven reinforcement learning can be reframed as a study of how agent memory heterogeneity shapes active learning performance in 2024-2025 SOTA agents. Intrinsic reward mechanisms that differ in how they encode unexpectedness act as implicit memory filters that prioritize novel state storage over redundant cached observations, creating a natural spectrum of curious agent behaviors that varies by how each method weights uncertainty in memory retention.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
